name: Arabic

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic
sources.notes: Consulted for the consonant inventory (28 phonemes, 4 emphatics ط ض ص ظ, pharyngeals ح ع), sun/moon letter assimilation as a synchronic coronal-place-driven rule, and MSA gemination via shadda.

sources.author: Thelwall, R. and Sa'Adeddin, M.A.
sources.title: Illustrations of the IPA: Arabic
sources.publisher: Journal of the International Phonetic Association, 20(2), 37-39
sources.notes: Cross-checked the emphatic/pharyngealized consonant set (sˤ, dˤ, tˤ, ðˤ) and glottal stop realisation against the JIPA reference illustration inventory catalogued in PHOIBLE.

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties

notes: Modern Standard Arabic (MSA). Inherits its grapheme/allophone inventory from Classical Arabic (arb) via graphemes_base/allophones_base, with one MSA-specific override: ض is transcribed [dˤ] (emphatic alveolar stop), not the Classical lateral-fricative [ɮˤ] Sibawayhi describes and arb.json retains — MSA has merged ض onto the plosive series (Watson 2002; Standard_Arabic_phonology). INPUT CONTRACT / KNOWN LIMITATION: this spec assumes fully-diacritized (fully tashkeel-marked) input. Standard written MSA text omits short-vowel diacritics (fatḥa/ḍamma/kasra/sukūn/shadda/tanwīn) almost everywhere outside the Quran, children's books, and pedagogical material; undiacritized input is NOT disambiguated by this spec (no morphological/statistical diacritic-restoration is implemented) and will transcribe incorrectly or incompletely wherever short vowels or gemination are orthographically absent. Gemination (shadda, ّ) is only encoded when explicitly marked in the input, via the inherited ّ → ː mapping from arb. sandhi_rules is inherited directly from arb (AR_SUN_ASSIMILATION, AR_HAMZAT_WASL, AR_PAUSAL_TANWIN) with no leaf-level override; AR_SUN_ASSIMILATION only fires between whitespace-separated tokens and therefore does NOT trigger on standard Arabic orthography, where the definite article ال- is written attached to the following word as one token — see docs/languages/ar.md and the rule's own notes for detail. No stress block is declared: MSA word stress is quantity-sensitive (driven by syllable weight, not written vowel-length-independent orthography) and highly register/dialect-variable in connected speech, so it is not reliably orthography-predictable; this exempts the spec from the research-tier stress requirement per docs/quality_tiers.md. Gold benchmark: CUNY-CL/wikipron 'ara_arab_broad.tsv' (Wiktionary-sourced broad IPA transcriptions, community-curated, ~17.5k pairs) registered as the 'ar' entry of the 'wikipron' dataset in scripts/benchmark.py, matching the precedent already used for gl/es/pt in this project.
